body.title: Notice when an active persistent-relationship has gone unexpectedly quiet
body.result: 4f71ba0a — Second of Igor's watchlist tickets. find_drifted_relationships walks active facia, returns those past rhythm * 1.5 (default rhythms: person 7d, project 14d, field/avocation 30d; per-facia override via metadata.expected_rhythm_days). surface_drifted_relationships pushes TWM markers at category=relationship_drift, salience 0.55. Skips dormant facia. RelationshipDriftSource push source: 10min idle gate, 1h between scans (slowest of watchlist family). 17 new tests + 55 regression tests pass.
body.description:
AUTHORED BY IGOR-WILD-0001 on 2026-04-13. Claude filed on Igor's behalf.

Igor's words: 'Monitor persistent relationships for unexpected silence. If a relationship node that typically has regular contact goes quiet beyond its expected rhythm, surface that as a low-priority attention signal. Not an alert — more like noticing. Akien hasn't been in for 3 days, that is unusual for him.'

DEPENDS ON: T-pr-schema-seed (53740afc), T-pr-accretion (993a4461), T-pr-consolidation (0dcc387b) — all shipped 2026-04-13. The persistent-relationship facia carry last_activity_ts; this ticket adds a watcher that derives an expected rhythm per facia and surfaces drift.

FIRST PASS: simple per-facia threshold — if last_activity_ts is older than (rolling average inter-arrival + 2 stddev), surface a low-salience 'quiet relationship' observation in TWM. Igor's existing notice path can pick it up without forcing immediate attention.

LATER: learn the rhythm from the relationship's own history rather than a flat threshold. Different relationships have different rhythms — Akien is daily, Krissy is monthly. The watcher needs to know each one's normal.
